headlines.description: Goals from Nicolas Jackson and Enzo Fernandez helped Chelsea to a 2-1 Premier League win at Leicester City on Saturday to climb into third-place in the table.
headlines.shortLinkText: Jackson, Fernandez net as Chelsea beat Leicester

events.name: Brighton & Hove Albion at AFC Bournemouth

headlines.description: Brighton & Hove Albion beat Bournemouth 2-1 at the Vitality Stadium in the Premier League on Saturday with goals from Joao Pedro and Kaoru Mitoma, while the visitors were reduced to 10 men when Carlos Baleba was sent off in the second half.
headlines.shortLinkText: Bournemouth vs Brighton: Pedro, Mitoma on target as visitors win

headlines.description: Arsenal returned to winning ways in the Premier League with Bukayo Saka and captain Martin Odegaard inspiring a comfortable 3-0 home victory against Nottingham Forest on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Arsenal thrash Forest to return to winning ways

headlines.description: Ross Barkley headed in a late equaliser as Aston Villa salvaged a 2-2 Premier League draw with lowly Crystal Palace on a wet and windy Saturday afternoon at Villa Park.
headlines.shortLinkText: Aston Villa vs Crystal Palace: Ross Barkley header saves draw

headlines.description: Ten-man Brentford picked up a first away point of the Premier League season following a 0-0 draw with toothless Everton at Goodison Park on Saturday, as the home side wasted numerous opportunities to score.
headlines.shortLinkText: Everton held to scoreless draw by 10-man Brentford

headlines.description: Matheus Cunha's superb double earned Wolves a 4-1 comeback victory at Fulham in the Premier League on Saturday, extending the visitors' unbeaten run to four games.
headlines.shortLinkText: Cunha's sublime double gives Wolves 4-1 comeback win at Fulham

headlines.description: Manchester City's misery continued with a shock 4-0 Premier League rout by Tottenham Hotspur at Etihad Stadium on Saturday, marking the first time in Pep Guardiola's dazzling managerial career that he has lost five games in a row across all competitions.
headlines.shortLinkText: City thrashed by Spurs for 5th consecutive loss
